main_question: In September 1441, Sibyl, who was wife of Richard de Hokynhull, in her own person sued William de Stanley, William de Orell, chaplain, William, son of William de Orell, bastard, Robert de Moldeworth, James de Eulowe, Thomas Glegge, and James le Bridde, chaplain, for one-third of eighteen messuages, 340 acres of land, 10 acres of meadow, 20 acres of pasture, 10 acres of wood, & 5s. 5d. rents in Chircheshotewik and Hokynhull, which she claimed in the King's court as her dower from the said Richard.
